# ThriveStack Product Telemetry — Step 1 of 3: User & Account Events
# Version: 1.0
# Hosted at: https://docs.thrivestack.ai/ai-setup/product/scoped/auth/llms.txt

---

## PURPOSE

This file gives AI coding assistants complete, executable instructions for instrumenting
user and account lifecycle events with ThriveStack. This is Step 1 of 3.

Events covered in this step:
  identify, group, signed_up, signed_in, signed_out,
  account_created, account_added_user, invite_sent, abuse check (optional)

Onboarding events and feature events are covered in Steps 2 and 3.
All event schemas are non-exhaustive. Additional properties beyond those listed are always allowed.

---

## HOW TO USE THIS GUIDE

Work through these phases in order. Do not write any code until Phase 0 is complete.

### Phase 0 — Understand the codebase first

1. Determine if the codebase is **frontend/full-stack** or **backend-only**:
   - If it runs in a browser or has a frontend entry point (Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Angular, Svelte, plain HTML), continue with this guide as written.
   - If it is a **backend/server-side application with no browser context** (Node.js API server, Express, Fastify, Python/Django/FastAPI/Flask, Go service, Ruby on Rails API, etc.):
     Read https://docs.thrivestack.ai/ai-setup/product/backend/calls.txt now, then continue with this guide.
     Apply these two differences throughout:
     - Replace every `thriveStack.identify/group/track([...])` call with the `thrivestack('identify/group/track', [...])` HTTP helper pattern from that file
     - Replace every `if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return` guard with nothing — that guard is frontend-only
     - Add `"source": "product"` to every `context` block
     Everything else in this guide — event schemas, firing order, questions to ask, verification checklist — applies unchanged.

2. **STOP** and ask the developer all three questions in a single message. Do not ask them separately. Do not proceed until all three are answered.
   > "Before I start, I have three quick questions:
   >
   > **1.** Does your app have **accounts or organizations** that multiple users can belong to (B2B), or is each user their own independent account with no shared org/account entity (B2C)?
   >
   > **2.** Do you have **European customers**? If yes, I'll instrument in EU compliance mode — user PII (email, name) will be excluded from all events globally, and group/account data will still be sent.
   >
   > **3.** During signup, do you want to check for abusive or disposable email addresses?
   > - **(a) Block** — prevent the signup and show an error message
   > - **(b) Flag** — allow signup but mark the user for manual review
   > - **(c) Skip** — do not implement abuse checking"

   Apply answers as follows:
   - B2B → proceed with full group hierarchy search (STEP 2)
   - B2C → skip hierarchy search, use B2C fallback directly (see STEP 2)
   - EU customers yes → activate EU compliance mode (see EU COMPLIANCE MODE section)
   - EU customers no → send all fields as documented
   - Abuse (c) → omit `checkAbuseEmail` entirely and mark row 9 as N/A in the summary

4. Search the codebase for auth handlers: signup/registration, login, logout, and invite flows.
   Then apply the following rule:
   - **None found**: STOP. Tell the developer exactly:
     > "I couldn't find signup, login, or logout handlers in this repository. Your auth code may live in a different repo. Run this same prompt in the repo where those handlers are, then return here if the main app also reads userId or groupId."
   - **Some but not all found**: Instrument what is present. For each flow not found, note in the summary: "not in this repo — run prompt there."
   - **All found**: proceed.

5. Identify the tech stack, auth library, and state management.
6. Find `userId` — see STEP 2 for search patterns and rules.
7. Find `groupId` — only if B2B; see STEP 2 for hierarchy, search patterns, and fallbacks.

### Phase 1 — Install the script tag
Add once to the root layout. Follow STEP 1 exactly for the detected framework.

### Phase 2 — Instrument lifecycle events
Follow the FIRING ORDER section exactly for each flow found.

### Phase 3 — Verify then summarise
Run through the VERIFICATION CHECKLIST. Fix anything missing. Then output the summary table.
After the summary is printed, proceed to Phase 4. Do not make any further code edits.

### Phase 4 — Live verification
Prompt the developer to verify events are flowing. See PHASE 4 section for the exact prompt and steps.

---

**REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT — strictly follow this, no exceptions.**
Do NOT write a narrative summary. Do NOT output a "Summary of Changes" section. Do NOT describe what you did in prose.
After instrumentation is complete, output the table below inline in the chat, then immediately proceed to Phase 4 — do not stop after the table.

```
ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary — Step 1: User & Account Events
X/11 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 0  | script tag | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Exact src used |
| 1  | identify | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Field used, file |
| 2  | group | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entity levels, billing root, file |
| 3  | signed_up | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 4  | signed_in | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 5  | signed_out | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file |
| 6  | account_created | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 7  | account_added_user | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Entry points covered |
| 8  | invite_sent | ✅/⚠️/❌ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9  | abuse_check | ✅/⚠️/❌ | block / flag / N/A |
| 10 | EU compliance | ✅ active / ➖ N/A | PII stripped / hash applied / not applicable |

User ID: [field] in [file]
Group hierarchy: [e.g. Organization → Account (billing root) → Workspace] or [B2C fallback]
Missing from this repo: [list events not found with reason, or "None"]
Unresolved: [anything skipped and why, or "None"]
```

---

## STEP 1 — INSTALL THE SCRIPT TAG

This is the ONLY initialization method. There is no npm package. There is no init() call.

Add once to the root layout file. Never inside a component or page.

### Next.js App Router (app/layout.tsx)
```tsx
import Script from 'next/script'
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html>
      <head>
        <Script
          src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
          strategy="afterInteractive"
          api-key={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY}
          data-source="product"
        />
      </head>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  )
}
```

### React (public/index.html)
```html
<script
  src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
  api-key="%REACT_APP_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY%"
  source="product">
</script>
```

### Vue / Nuxt / Plain HTML
```html
<script
  src="https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js"
  api-key="YOUR_ENV_VAR_HERE"
  source="product">
</script>
```

Rules:
- Write the API key to the environment variable file appropriate for this stack (.env, .env.local, application.yml, etc.) — never in source code
- `api-key` must reference that environment variable — never hardcoded
- `source="product"` — always this exact string, never change it
- Global object after load: `thriveStack` — camelCase, lowercase 's'
- Place in root layout ONCE only

---

## STEP 2 — FIND userId AND groupId

### userId
Must be a stable unique identifier that does not change across sessions.
May be a UUID, integer, or any format the auth provider uses.
Prefer a stable unique ID over email. Email is acceptable as `user_id` if it is the only stable identifier available — use it without blocking.

Search patterns:
```javascript
const { user } = useAuth()               → user.id
const session = useSession()             → session.user.id
useContext(AuthContext)                  → context.user.id
const { userId } = auth()               → userId  (Clerk)
store.auth.user.id                       → (Redux/Zustand)
claims.sub, claims.user_id              → (JWT)
```

If no stable identifier found — stop and ask:
> "What field uniquely identifies a user in your database — their ID, not their email?"

---

### groupId — Entity Hierarchy

Account is always mandatory — it is the billing/contractual root entity.
All other levels are additional and each gets its own `group()` call.

Default hierarchy:
```
Organization   (brand/company umbrella)
  └── Account  ← MANDATORY — billing root
        └── Tenant
              └── Workspace
                    └── Team
```

This is a default — if the developer confirms a different entity is their billing root,
treat that as Account regardless of what it is called in code.

Search patterns:
```
AUTH CONTEXT:
  user.accountId / user.account.id
  user.orgId / user.organizationId / user.organization.id
  user.tenantId / user.workspaceId / user.companyId
  session.organization.id / session.account.id

JWT CUSTOM CLAIMS:
  claims.org_id / claims.account_id / claims.tenant_id
  claims['https://yourapp.com/org_id']   ← Auth0 pattern

STORE (Redux/Zustand/Pinia):
  store.currentOrg.id / store.activeWorkspace.id
  useOrganization().organization.id

POST-LOGIN API RESPONSE:
  loginResponse.organization.id / meResponse.current_organization.id

URL PARAMS:
  /app/:accountId / /org/:orgId / /workspace/:workspaceId
  Note: if only in URL params, ask developer if always available
```

B2C / no-org fallback:
In B2C there is no account entity — each user is their own "account". There is no `account_created` event.
`group()` fires in the same handler as `signed_up`, registering the user themselves as the group.

```javascript
group_id       = user.id         // same as userId
account_name   = user.name       // user's full name
account_domain = user.email      // full email address — not just the domain part
// omit plan_id and plan_name entirely
```
`group()` fires once at first registration — never again.

Stop and ask if:
- No org-like field found after exhaustive search
- Multiple top-level entities exist and billing root is unclear
- groupId only in URL params and may not always be available

Never proceed without groupId. Never generate a fake ID.

**What is NOT a valid groupId:**
- A company name string (`user.company`, `"Acme Corp"`) — this is a name, not an ID
- An email domain (`user.email.split('@')[1]`, `"acme.com"`) — this is a domain, not an ID
- `account_domain`, `account_name`, or any human-readable string
- A generated UUID (`uuidv4()`) — IDs must come from the auth context

If the only org-related field available is a name or domain string (e.g. the app stores `{ firstName, lastName, email, company, role }` with no org ID), **stop and ask**:
> "I found a `company` field but it appears to be a name, not a unique ID. What field in your database uniquely identifies an account or organization — their ID, not their name?"

### Storing groupId for use across handlers

Once you identify where `groupId` (the Account-level ID) comes from, store it in a variable that is accessible from all auth handlers — not just the signup handler.

Common patterns:
```javascript
// Module-level variable updated after login/signup
let groupId = null

async function handleSignup() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signUp(...)
  groupId = org.id   // ← store here
  // ... thriveStack calls use groupId
}

async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  groupId = org.id   // ← update here
  // ... thriveStack.track([{ ..., "context": { "group_id": groupId } }])
}

async function handleLogout() {
  // groupId is still in scope — use it before clearing
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_out", ..., "context": { "group_id": groupId } }])
  groupId = null
}
```

If `groupId` comes from a React context, Redux store, or session object, read it from there in every handler — do not assume it flows automatically. Confirm it resolves to a non-null value before firing any `track()` call.

### Context switching
When user switches active workspace/tenant/org:
- Do NOT re-fire `group()`
- Update `context.group_id` to the new entity's ID for all subsequent `track()` calls

---

## STEP 3 — identify()

`identify()` registers the user's identity with ThriveStack. It is the user equivalent of `group()` — it tells ThriveStack who this user is.

Fire it:
- **On signup** — when the user is first created
- **On login** — to re-establish the user's identity for the session

That is all that is required. Profile updates are optional: if more user information becomes available later (e.g. the user completes their profile), you may fire `identify()` again to update their traits in ThriveStack. But this is not required — its absence must never cause a warning or ⚠️ in the summary.

`user_id` is always mandatory. Send whatever traits are known at the time.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "traits": {
    "user_email": user.email,    // omit in EU mode
    "user_name": user.name       // omit in EU mode
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## STEP 4 — group()

`group()` registers the identity of a group entity with ThriveStack. It is the group equivalent of `identify()` for users.

Fire it wherever a new group entity is **created** in the codebase — not on login, not on context switch, not on invite acceptance.

- **Account** is mandatory — ThriveStack reports and metrics run at Account level. If you find no other entity levels, at minimum instrument the Account.
- **All other levels** (Organization, Tenant, Workspace, Team, BU, etc.) are optional. Instrument any you find, each with its own `group()` call and the appropriate `group_type`.
- One `group()` call per entity level found. Each gets its own `group_type`.

### Account (mandatory)
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": account.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_domain": account.domain,
    "account_name": account.name,
    "plan_id": account.plan?.id,      // omit if unavailable
    "plan_name": account.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": {
    "group_id": account.id,           // must match group_id exactly
    "group_type": "Account"
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

### Additional levels (same signature, change group_type and ID)
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": org.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Organization",     // "Organization"|"Tenant"|"Workspace"|"Team"|"BU" etc.
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "account_name": org.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id, "group_type": "Organization" },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

`"Account"` is reserved for the billing root only.

---

## STEP 5 — TRACK EVENTS

### Base signature
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "event_name_here",
  "user_id": userId,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## EVENT SCHEMAS

### signed_up
When: New user registration completes.
Context: Include if groupId available. Omit if account doesn't exist yet.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_up",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "utm_source": getUTMParam('utm_source'),
    "utm_medium": getUTMParam('utm_medium'),
    "utm_campaign": getUTMParam('utm_campaign'),
    "utm_term": getUTMParam('utm_term')
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.orgId },   // omit if not yet available
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])

function getUTMParam(param) {
  return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get(param)
    || sessionStorage.getItem(param)
    || null
}
```

---

### signed_in
When: Existing user logs in successfully.
No UTM params. `context` is mandatory — groupId is always available at login.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_in",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### signed_out
When: Existing user logs out.
`context` is mandatory — groupId is available at logout.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_out",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_created
When: A new account is first created in the app. Do not fire if a user is joining an existing account.

`account_created` is the lifecycle event — it tells ThriveStack *when* this happened.
`group()` is the identity call — it tells ThriveStack *who* this account is.

When you find where `account_created` fires, also call `group()` in the same handler before it — because this is where the account entity comes into existence and ThriveStack needs to learn its identity at that same moment.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
const now = Date.now()

// Register the account's identity with ThriveStack
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": org.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "plan_id": org.plan?.id,      // omit if unavailable
    "plan_name": org.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id, "group_type": "Account" },
  "timestamp": new Date(now).toISOString()
}])

// Track the account creation event
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_created",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "user_email": user.email,     // omit in EU mode
    "user_name": user.name,       // omit in EU mode
    "plan_name": org.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date(now + 1).toISOString()
}])

// The creator is automatically the first member — always fire account_added_user here
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_added_user",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,     // omit in EU mode
    "user_name": user.name,       // omit in EU mode
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "role": user.role,
    "join_method": "signup",
    "is_first_user": true
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date(now + 2).toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_added_user
When: A user joins an account for the first time, by any method.

This event is independent of `signed_up` — there is no fixed order between them:
- **New account created during signup**: the creator is automatically the first member. Fire `account_added_user` in the same handler as `account_created`, immediately after it. This is the mandatory minimum — if `account_created` is instrumented, `account_added_user` must be there too.
- In enterprise B2B: an admin may add a user to an account before that user has ever signed up — `account_added_user` fires first, `signed_up` fires later when the user activates
- In SSO/SCIM: the user may be provisioned with no `signed_up` event at all

This event can live in the frontend, backend, or both depending on how the app handles membership. It may also live in a different repo from the signup/login code. Search broadly:
- Frontend: post-signup flows, invite acceptance handlers, onboarding completion
- Backend: user provisioning endpoints, SCIM handlers, admin APIs, webhook handlers for SSO

Do NOT fire if the user is already a member of the account.

`join_method` values:
- `"signup"` — user created the account themselves
- `"invite"` — user accepted an invitation
- `"sso"` — provisioned via SSO
- `"scim"` — provisioned via SCIM
- `"admin"` — added directly by an admin

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_added_user",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "role": user.role,
    "join_method": "signup",      // "signup"|"invite"|"sso"|"scim"|"admin"
    "is_first_user": true         // true only if this user is the account's first member
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

If `account_added_user` handlers exist in a different repo, note it in the summary and tell the developer to run this prompt there.

---

### invite_sent
This event is optional — not all products have an invite flow. If no invite functionality is found in this repo, mark row 8 as N/A.

It may also live in a different repo (e.g. a separate team management service). If that's the case, note it in the summary.

When: A user sends a team invitation to another person.
Search for: `inviteUser` `sendInvite` `inviteTeamMember` — forms or handlers with email + role fields.
All properties beyond `user_email` and `invitee_email` are send-if-available.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "invite_sent",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "invitee_email": invitee.email,
    "invitee_role": invitee.role,
    "invitee_role_id": invitee.roleId,
    "invitee_user_id": invitee.userId,
    "invitee_team_id": invitee.teamId,
    "invitee_team": invitee.teamName,
    "source_url": window.location.href
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### checkAbuseEmail — Abuse Prevention
When: Before signup form submission completes.
Prerequisites: ThriveStack SDK script tag installed (STEP 1).

Use the answer from Phase 0 step 3 (block / flag / skip). If skip was chosen, omit this section entirely.

Place after the email input field and just before signup submission:

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
const checkAbuse = await thriveStack.checkAbuseEmail(userEmail)
if (checkAbuse.success && checkAbuse.data.isAbuse === 1) {
  // BLOCK path — prevent form submission and show error
  showError(checkAbuse.message)

  // FLAG path — allow signup but mark for review (use instead of block above)
  // flagUserForReview(userEmail, checkAbuse.data.reasonForAbuse)
}
```

Note: `await` is intentional here — this is a data check that gates the flow, not a fire-and-forget event.

`reasonForAbuse` fields: `isDisposable` (throwaway email provider), `domainExists` (false = domain not found), `isDuplicate` (email already seen in system).

---

## EU COMPLIANCE MODE

Activate this mode when the developer confirms they have European customers. Apply globally — do not attempt per-user detection.

### What to strip from every call

| Field | Action |
|---|---|
| `user_email` | Remove from all `identify()`, `track()`, and `group()` calls |
| `user_name` | Remove from all `identify()`, `track()`, and `group()` calls |
| `invitee_email` | Remove from `invite_sent` |
| `account_name`, `account_id` | Keep — business data, not personal |
| `account_domain` | **Keep in B2B** — it is the company's business domain (e.g. `"acme.com"`), not personal data. **Strip in B2C** — in B2C mode `account_domain = user.email`, which is personal data; remove it in that case |
| `user_role`, `join_method`, UTM params | Keep — not personal data |
| `source_url` | Leave as developer's responsibility |

### user_id when it is an email address

If the only stable identifier available is the user's email (B2C fallback or no ID field found), hash it with SHA-256 before using it as `user_id`. The hash is consistent — same email always produces the same hash — so ThriveStack can track the same user across sessions without storing the raw email.

Add this helper once, near your auth utilities:

```javascript
async function anonymiseUserId(email) {
  const encoded = new TextEncoder().encode(email.toLowerCase().trim())
  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', encoded)
  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer))
    .map(b => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0'))
    .join('')
}
```

Compute the hash once where you first obtain the user object, store it in a variable, then use that variable in all `thriveStack` calls. Do not call `anonymiseUserId` inline inside each thriveStack call.

```javascript
// Compute once at login/signup
const userId = await anonymiseUserId(user.email)

// Then use userId everywhere — no raw email in any thriveStack call
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": userId, "traits": {}, "timestamp": new Date().toISOString() }])
```

If the user_id is a UUID, integer, or any non-email stable ID — it is not PII. Send it as-is, no hashing needed.

### EU mode summary row

Add a row to the summary table output:

```
| EU | compliance mode | ✅ active / ➖ not applicable | PII stripped / hash applied if email-based ID |
```

---

## FIRING ORDER

`signed_up` and `account_added_user` are independent events — instrument each where it happens in the code. There is no fixed order between them. In enterprise B2B, `account_added_user` can precede `signed_up` (admin provisions the user first; user activates later). Do not force an order that doesn't match the codebase.

### Self-service signup — new account created
```
identify → group(Account) → [group(other levels if found)] → account_created → account_added_user
signed_up fires in the same signup handler, at the point registration completes
```

### Self-service signup — B2C (no account entity)
```
identify → group(userId as group_id) → signed_up
```

### Login
```
identify → signed_in
```

### Logout
```
signed_out
```

### User invited — accepts invite and signs up for the first time
```
identify → signed_up → account_added_user
(account_added_user fires at invite acceptance, not at invite send)
```

### User invited — already has an account, joins a new one
```
identify → signed_in → account_added_user
```

### User invited — already a member of this account
```
No events.
```

### Admin adds user / SSO / SCIM provisioning
```
account_added_user
(signed_up may fire later when the user activates — instrument it where activation happens)
```

---

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

Before delivering the summary, verify all 10 points. Fix anything missing first.

- [ ] 0. script tag — src is exactly `https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js` — no variation, no substitution. Known wrong URLs to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
- [ ] 1. identify — fires on signup and login. Profile update calls are optional — do not mark ⚠️ if no profile update handler exists.
- [ ] 2. group — open the file where the account entity is created and confirm `thriveStack.group()` with `group_type: "Account"` is physically present there. Do not mark ✅ based on reasoning — verify the call exists in the file. If absent, add it. Also confirm any additional entity levels (Org, Workspace, etc.) found have their own `group()` calls.
- [ ] 3. signed_up — correct flow, UTM captured
- [ ] 4. signed_in — fires on every login, context block present
- [ ] 5. signed_out — fires on logout, context block present
- [ ] 6. account_created — only when new account actually created
- [ ] 7. account_added_user — if account_created is instrumented, open that same handler and confirm account_added_user fires immediately after it with join_method: "signup" and is_first_user: true. If it is present, mark ✅ — do not add any caveat or "missing" note about SSO/SCIM/admin provisioning unless this repo actually contains SSO/SCIM/admin provisioning code. Only note other entry points if they exist in this repo and were not instrumented.
- [ ] 8. invite_sent — all invite handlers found
- [ ] 9. abuse_check — if chosen: checkAbuseEmail called before signup submission, block or flag implemented; if skipped: mark N/A
- [ ] 10. EU compliance — if active: scan every thriveStack call in the codebase and confirm no `user_email` or `user_name` field is present anywhere. Confirm `invitee_email` is absent from invite_sent. If user_id was email-based, confirm `anonymiseUserId` is applied. If B2C mode is active, also confirm `account_domain` is absent from all calls (it equals user.email in B2C and is PII). If B2B mode is active, `account_domain` should be present — confirm it was not accidentally stripped. If not active: mark ➖ N/A.

---

## PHASE 4 — VERIFY EVENTS ARE FLOWING

This phase is mandatory. Do not skip it. Do not print a completion message before running this phase.

Determine whether you have the capability to open a browser and inspect network requests. Then **STOP** and ask the developer, exactly:
> "Instrumentation is complete. How would you like to verify that events are reaching ThriveStack?
> - **(a) Manual Testing** — Run your app, open the browser Network tab, filter for `thrivestack`, and look for POST requests to `api.app.thrivestack.ai` — you should see `identify`, `group`, and `track` calls with your user data
> - **(b) AI Enabled Testing** — Open your app and I will validate the events are firing for you"

Only include option **(b)** if you have the capability to open a browser and inspect network requests. If you cannot, present only **(a)**.
Do not proceed until the developer has answered.

**If (a) — Manual Testing:**
Walk the developer through step by step:
1. Start the app and open it in a browser
2. Open DevTools → Network tab → filter by `thrivestack`
3. Perform a signup, then a login
4. Confirm POST requests appear for `identify` and `track` at `api.app.thrivestack.ai/api/`
5. Click each request and verify the payload contains `event_name`, `user_id`, and `context.group_id`

If any requests are missing or the developer reports errors: ask them to share the failing request's URL, status code, and payload. Cross-reference against this entire guide — event schemas, firing order, hard rules, and verification checklist, identify the issue, fix the instrumentation, and ask the developer to verify again.

**If (b) — AI Enabled Testing:**
1. Ask for the local dev URL (e.g. `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Open the app using your browser capability
3. Walk through the signup and login flows
4. Confirm network requests to `api.app.thrivestack.ai/api/identify` and `api.app.thrivestack.ai/api/track` fire with correct payloads
5. If any requests are missing or returning errors, inspect the payload, cross-reference against this entire guide, fix the instrumentation, and re-verify
6. Report which events fired and which did not

After verification is complete, print the following:

```
Step 1 verified. Your user and account events are now flowing into ThriveStack.

Next steps:
- View your events: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Check setup progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Run diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report

When ready, continue with Step 2 — Onboarding Events:
→ https://docs.thrivestack.ai/public/product/setup/advanced-ai-instrumentation.html
```

---

## HARD RULES

1. Script src must be exactly `https://cdn.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/script.js` — never alter the URL. Known wrong substitutions to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
2. Global object: `thriveStack` — never `ThriveStack` or `thrivestack`
3. Every method takes an array: `identify([{...}])` `group([{...}])` `track([{...}])`
4. Guard every call: `if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return`
5. timestamp: `new Date().toISOString()` on every call — when multiple events fire in the same handler, use `const now = Date.now()` once and offset each by 1ms: `new Date(now).toISOString()`, `new Date(now + 1).toISOString()`, etc.
6. `group_type: "Account"` reserved for billing root only
7. `context.group_id` must equal `group_id` on group() — same variable always
8. `context` required on every `track()` — except `signed_up` when groupId not yet available
9. userId and groupId from auth context only — never hardcode, never generate
10. `api-key` from environment variable only — never in source code
11. Fire-and-forget — never `await` thriveStack calls (exception: `checkAbuseEmail` which intentionally uses await)
12. Fire after action succeeds — inside `.then()` or after `await`
13. `group()` fires where a new entity is created — never on login, never on context switch, never on invite acceptance
14. `account_added_user` fires in all entry points — signup, invite, SSO/SCIM/admin
15. Do not fire `account_added_user` if user is already a member
16. `user_added_to_account` does not exist — use `account_added_user`
17. Additional properties beyond documented ones are always permitted
18. Stop after the summary is output — do not fix bugs, refactor code, clean up existing logic, or make any other changes outside of ThriveStack instrumentation. Your only permitted modifications are the ThriveStack calls added in this guide.
19. Never remove the ThriveStack script tag — once added, it must remain in `<head>` permanently. Any subsequent edit must preserve it. After every edit, verify the script tag is still present; if it was removed, restore it immediately before continuing.
20. EU compliance is global — when active, strip `user_email` and `user_name` from every single thriveStack call without exception. Do not apply it selectively per event or per user.
21. Never hash user_id unless it is confirmed to be an email address — do not hash UUIDs, integers, or opaque IDs.

---

## CRITICAL MISTAKES

WRONG — missing array:
```javascript
thriveStack.track({ "event_name": "signed_up" })
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_up" }])
```

WRONG — no guard:
```javascript
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```

WRONG — group() on login:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": org.id }])
}
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id, ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_in", ... }])
}
```

WRONG — account_created without account_added_user (first user membership never recorded):
```javascript
async function handleSignup() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signUp(...)
  thriveStack.group([{ ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_created", ... }])
  // account_added_user missing — the first user is never recorded as a member
}
```
RIGHT — all three fire together when a new account is created:
```javascript
async function handleSignup() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signUp(...)
  const now = Date.now()
  thriveStack.group([{ ..., "timestamp": new Date(now).toISOString() }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_created", ..., "timestamp": new Date(now + 1).toISOString() }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_added_user", "properties": { "join_method": "signup", "is_first_user": true, ... }, "timestamp": new Date(now + 2).toISOString() }])
}
```

WRONG — account_created without group() (account identity never registered with ThriveStack):
```javascript
async function handleSignup() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signUp(...)
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_created", ... }])
}
```
RIGHT — group() registers the account identity; account_created records the event. Both belong where the account is created:
```javascript
async function handleSignup() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signUp(...)
  const now = Date.now()
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": org.id, "traits": { "group_type": "Account", ... }, "context": { "group_id": org.id, "group_type": "Account" }, "timestamp": new Date(now).toISOString() }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "account_created", ..., "timestamp": new Date(now + 1).toISOString() }])
}
```

WRONG — using a name or domain string as group_id:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.company }])          // "Acme Corp" — a name, not an ID
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.email.split('@')[1] }]) // "acme.com" — a domain, not an ID
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": account.account_domain }])   // domain field, not an ID
```
RIGHT — stop and ask the developer:
> "I found a `company` field but it appears to be a name, not a unique ID. What field in your database uniquely identifies an account or organization — their ID, not their name?"

WRONG — generating groupId:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": uuidv4() }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.orgId }])
```

WRONG — context.group_id mismatch:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": uuidv4() }
}])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": org.id }
}])
```

WRONG — missing context.group_id on track() calls (groupId not carried into handler):
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id, ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_in", "user_id": user.id, "properties": { ... } }])
  // context block missing — ThriveStack cannot associate this event with an account
}
```
RIGHT — read groupId from wherever the login response exposes it and include context:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  const groupId = org.id   // read from login response, session, store, or auth context
  thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id, ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{
    "event_name": "signed_in",
    "user_id": user.id,
    "properties": { ... },
    "context": { "group_id": groupId },
    "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
  }])
}
```

WRONG — EU mode B2C: account_domain is user.email, treated as business data:
```javascript
// B2C + EU mode — account_domain holds user.email, must be stripped
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": user.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_domain": user.email,   // ← PII in B2C, must be removed in EU mode
    "account_name": user.name
  }
}])
```
RIGHT — in B2C + EU mode, strip account_domain entirely:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": user.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account"
    // account_domain omitted — it equals user.email in B2C and is PII
    // account_name also omitted — it equals user.name and is PII
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.id, "group_type": "Account" },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```
